runs.name: Intervals 4x800 with 1600 warm up and cool down
Carrera a la hora del almuerzo

adaptivePlanCache.narrative: Your biometrics show significant recovery concerns: resting HR is 12 bpm above baseline (64 vs 52) and wrist temp is massively elevated (+95.7°C is clearly a sensor error, but any deviation flags stress). Recent runs show you're running threshold effort (HR 165-171) when trying to go easy. This week we prioritize genuine aerobic development at true Zone 2 and manage fatigue.
adaptivePlanCache.weeklyFocus: Establish true easy pace discipline (HR 130-145) and monitor recovery markers daily
adaptivePlanCache.autoRegulationRule: If resting HR remains >60 bpm tomorrow, convert Thursday intervals to easy run and reduce all lift volumes by 1 set

bench.coachNote: Last session 190×5@7 was strong. Small jump to build on that momentum. Stop set immediately if bar speed slows.

ohp.coachNote: Conservative start given no recent data. Focus on keeping elbows forward and full lockout overhead.

squat.coachNote: Moderate load to assess movement quality. Hit depth every rep, maintain neutral spine throughout.

dl.coachNote: Same as squat weight—this is intentional given fatigue markers. Film a set to check setup and lockout pattern.

tuesday.coachNote: This will feel SLOW. Walk if HR climbs above 145. The goal is actual aerobic adaptation, not cardiovascular fatigue. Check HR every 5 minutes.

thursday.coachNote: Converted from intervals due to elevated resting HR. You need aerobic base work more than speed right now. No intervals until recovery markers normalize.

saturday.coachNote: Reduced from typical long run volume. Start at 12:30 pace minimum. If HR drifts above 150 after mile 3, slow down further or walk 1 min every mile.

adaptivePlanCache.recoveryFlag: ELEVATED RESTING HR (+12 bpm above baseline) — all runs must stay true easy pace with strict HR caps. Monitor daily and report any fatigue, soreness, or motivation drops.

primaryRecommendation.keyInstruction: Slow way down—your recent easy runs are consistently 15-25 bpm too hot for aerobic development

alternatives.condition: if HR climbs above 145 bpm despite slow pace
alternatives.recommendation: Cut to 2 miles and add 1-2 min walk breaks every 0.5 mile to keep HR under 145

alternatives.condition: if resting HR elevated >8 bpm above 52
alternatives.recommendation: Easy walk 2-3 miles at conversational effort instead

watchOutFor: Resting HR currently 60 bpm (baseline 52)—you're carrying fatigue from the 9-day training gap
Every 'easy' run since April has averaged 151-174 HR—way above Zone 2, limiting aerobic base building
Your 10:07 pace on 5/16 spiked HR to 171—need 11:30+ pace to stay aerobic
reminders: Check HR at 0.5 mile—if >145, slow down immediately
2026-05-19.coachNote: Your easy runs are tempo efforts in disguise. Resting HR 8 bpm elevated signals incomplete recovery. To hit sub-20 5K, you need true aerobic base work at 130-145 bpm, even if it feels painfully slow. Slow down to speed up long-term.
